task: Audit 31 losing trades to determine hard SL vs guardian exit, compute structural SL savings
local_output: 10 trades hit hard SL, 9 would survive with structural SL
opus_correction: Step 1: Query 31 losing trades. Step 2: Identify 10 that hit hard SL. Step 3: Pull M15 candles from OANDA. Step 4: Compute EMA100 structural SL = max(3xATR, EMA100 + buffer). Step 5: 9/10 survive. Structural SL (E100) won 6/9, ATR-3x floor won 3/9. One coin-flip at exact boundary. 171.8 pips / $798.91 saved.
opus_reasoning: The structural SL approach using EMA100 as a floor provides significantly more breathing room than pure ATR-based stops. The key insight is that in trending markets, EMA100 distance from entry naturally widens, giving trades more room exactly when they need it most. The 3xATR floor ensures minimum protection in ranging conditions.

task: Nightly learning sentry Apr 6 — trade performance and pipeline health
opus_correction: First, queried trade_log.db for closed trades and 7-day window. 54.4% WR but severe R:R inversion (losses 3-4x larger than wins). Failure attribution: 25% dead market entries (BB < 8 pips), V4_EARLY_WARNING worst setup at -73.5 net pips. Critical: finding_id NULL on 100% of trades for 10+ days — learning loop completely broken.
opus_reasoning: Win rate above 50% with negative net pips means the system needs tighter loss management, not better entry selection. The finding_id NULL regression is the highest priority fix because it blocks all downstream learning — condition effectiveness, stale watch cleanup, and weak condition filtering are all blind without trade-to-watch linkage.

task: Nightly learning sentry Apr 3 — performance and pipeline diagnostics
local_output: 5 trades, 60% WR, +3.6 pips, multiple pipeline regressions
opus_correction: Phase 1: Query trades. Phase 2: Win rate 60% (3W/2L). Phase 3: Failure attribution — dead market + weak conditions. Phase 4: finding_id NULL on 100% of 91 trades over 7 days. Phase 5: Indicator coverage regressed to 40%. Phase 6: Flight recorder DB empty — learning loops offline.
opus_reasoning: Three simultaneous pipeline regressions (finding_id, indicator coverage, flight recorder) suggest a common root cause — likely a deployment that broke the data enrichment pipeline. The indicator coverage regression from a supposedly-fixed state implies the fix was either reverted or bypassed by a parallel code path.
